qas.q: Who fills the DS role for the Marine Div, MLG, MAW?
What are the 6 functions of Logistics
qas.a:
I&L (Installations & Logistics) - I&L shapes logistics plans and policies to sustain excellence in war fighting

DLA (Defense Logistics Agency) - Department of Defense's largest logistics combat support agency, providing worldwide logistics support in both peacetime and wartime to the military services as well as several civilian agencies and foreign countries

qas.q: Who provides non-organic logistic support for the MEF, REG, BN?
qas.a: The activity which actually provides services and supplies to the combat forces. Since most of the delivery of resources occurs at the tactical level of war, combat service support has been considered to be essentially the same as tactical logistics.

qas.q: Who provides ground support to the MAG/MAW?
qas.a:
a) Responsiveness
b) Simplicity
c) Flexibility
d) Economy
e) Sustainability
f) Survivability
g) Attainability

qas.q: Which organizations are involved in the strategic level of logistics and their roles?

qas.a:
"Total Force Structure Management System" (TFSMS) is a system that combines manpower and equipment data for the purpose of managing the "total force". It's mission is "to serve as the primary data source and business engine for the activities defined in MCO 5311.1E"

where you go to access TO&E stuff

qas.q: What are the 7 principles of logistics support?
What unit provides DS to an infantry division
qas.a: the effective support of tactical-level logistics through activity at the operational/strategic levels

qas.q: What is TFSMS, its role and purpose?
qas.a: "mission-essential task" - tasks that must be doable well in combat; the essence of a community's existence; core skills and unit capabilities

qas.q: What unit provides GS to Motor T?
qas.a: "mission-essential task list" - list of tasks that maintain proficiency in the skills required to accomplish a unit's mission

a) Strategic: resources are organized, trained, and equipped; also developed

b) Operational: links tactical and strategic; gets the strategic resources to the places they can be used tactically

c) Tactical: CSS, 6 Functions
provides general/intermediate maintenance/support for tactical ordnance, engineering, motor T, comms, and general support equipment

qas.q: Who provides engineer support to the MARDIV?
qas.a: 0402 Logistics Officers plan and coordinate all 6 functional areas of logistics

qas.q: What are the 3 Levels of War and their Logistical roles?
qas.a: the impetus/stimulus of logistics is from the rear, directly to the using unit

qas.q: What are the capabilities of a maintenance battalion?
What MOS plans the 6 functions of logistics?
qas.a: the TO&E is a list of personnel, billets, and equipment assigned to a unit. It includes the unit's mission and essential tasks and the number of above things that the unit "rates"

qas.q: Know where the impetus of the Marine Corps comes from
qas.a: military ships with supplies required for rapid resupply to forward-deployed troop

qas.q: Which unit provides aviation maintenance and support to the MAW?
What is the purpose of the TO&E?
qas.a:
a) 0431, Logistics/Embarkation Specialist - prepares supplies for embarkation

b) 0491, Logistics Mobility Chief - coordinates/supervises embarkation, landing support

c) 0481, Landing Support Specialist - supports establishment/control of throughput on beaches, LZ's, etc

d) 0430, Mobility Officer - plan/execute movements of personnel/equipment via all modes of transport

e) maybe 0405 (aerial delivery officer) and 0451 (airborne delivery specialist)?

qas.q: What is a maritime pre-positioning force?
qas.a:
The tactical level of war is the level of war at which battles and engagements are planned and executed to achieve military objectives assigned to tactical units or task forces.

Reference: MCTP 3-40B.
